title: parserParity.test.ts legitimises a TS↔Rust output divergence on empty/whitespace input that contradicts spec REQ-FEED-025 non-empty invariant
description: behavioral-spec.md lines 716–723 (REQ-FEED-025 amendment) states the Rust parse_markdown_to_blocks 'non-empty invariant' is 'consistent with the TS parseMarkdownToBlocks 最低 1 ブロック保持 invariant (docs/domain/aggregates.md §1)'. parserParity.test.ts:64-70 asserts the OPPOSITE: that TS parseMarkdownToBlocks('') returns Ok([]) (length 0). The implementation it imports (src/lib/domain/capture-auto-save/parse-markdown-to-blocks.ts:78-82) confirms this — it short-circuits on `markdown.trim() === ''` and returns Ok([]). One of three things is true and ALL of them are unresolved: (a) the spec's claim that TS preserves a non-empty invariant is factually wrong and the spec needs amendment (then PROP-FEED-S4-016 cannot claim cross-language parity for the empty-input branch and should explicitly carve it out); (b) the chosen $lib implementation diverges from the canonical TS reference at docs/domain/code/ts/src/shared/blocks.ts (which only declares the type signature; no runtime is committed in the canonical doc — but the doc forwards to aggregates.md §1 which the spec quotes as guaranteeing 'minimum 1 block'), in which case the production parser is non-conformant and emitting Ok([]) for blank bodies is a runtime spec violation; (c) the spec is internally consistent and the test should fail on the empty branch instead of asserting `toHaveLength(0)`. The test as written documents a known divergence with a comment ('intentional and spec-acknowledged') but no spec section actually acknowledges it; the spec asserts equivalence. This is grade-inflation by comment.
remediation: Resolve one of: (1) Amend behavioral-spec.md REQ-FEED-025 to declare empty-input behavior as deliberately divergent and document why (e.g., TS empty-array is what Vault hydration consumes; Rust paragraph('') is what the editor channel needs; the conversion happens at the IPC boundary). Then update parserParity.test.ts comment to cite the new spec line, not invent its own justification. (2) Change parserParity.test.ts to import a TS implementation that mirrors the Rust non-empty invariant (single empty paragraph), making it the canonical TS runtime for IPC parity purposes — separate from the Vault-Hydration-time TS parser. (3) Most rigorous: add a single TS adapter `parseMarkdownToBlocksForIpc(s: string)` that wraps the existing parser and emits the Rust-equivalent non-empty form, and have parserParity.test.ts test THAT function. Whichever option is chosen, the comment 'intentional divergence and spec-acknowledged' must be backed by an actual spec citation, not by the test's own narrative. Until then, PROP-FEED-S4-016 is proven only for the single canonical fixture and not for the broader cross-language equivalence the spec claims.
